title: winey, crispy Vietnamese hazelnuts PHO
description: A date-night Vietnamese dish featuring shelled hazelnuts and double-rise bread. This recipe makes a winey, crispy, and indulgent PHO.
instructions: Heat 1 tablespoon of vegetable oil in a large stockpot over medium-high heat.
Add the sliced onion and cook until caramelized, about 8 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and grated ginger and cook for an additional 1-2 minutes, until fragrant.
Add the pho broth, fish sauce, soy sauce, and sugar to the stockpot and bring to a boil.
Reduce the heat to low and simmer for 10 minutes, skimming any impurities from the surface.
While the broth is simmering, toast the hazelnuts in a separate pan over medium heat, stirring frequently, until crispy and fragrant.
Cut the double-rise bread into 1-inch slices and toast until lightly browned.
To assemble the PHO, place a few slices of toasted bread into a bowl, then add slices of toasted hazelnuts, and finally ladle the hot pho broth over the top.
